description: Offline-first graph of Studio systems, project modules, asset indexes, technology indexes, and planned dependency-aware development phases.

graphModel.direction: source depends on target unless edgeType says feeds, powers, or indexes

nodes.label: Studio Dashboard
nodes.description: Central Studio launchpad and UI shell.

nodes.label: Knowledge Engine
nodes.description: Common index model and searchable registry for Studio records.

nodes.label: Asset Library
nodes.description: Offline-first asset schema and catalog for reusable files.

nodes.label: Technology Vault
nodes.description: Commercial-safety technology catalog and implementation pipeline.

nodes.label: Creator Suite
nodes.description: Browser-based creative tools for sprites, drawing, GIFs, prompts, and characters.

nodes.label: DataByteSprites
nodes.description: Flagship discovery, capture, battle, codex, and worldbuilding project.

nodes.label: Simulation Lab
nodes.description: Sandbox for game mechanics, AI workflows, experiments, and world systems.

nodes.label: Studio Modules Index
nodes.description: Module and index registry used by Studio search.

nodes.label: Asset Library Index
nodes.description: Asset catalog consumed by the unified Studio search engine.

nodes.label: Technology Vault Index
nodes.description: Technology catalog consumed by the unified Studio search engine.

nodes.label: Studio Global Search
nodes.description: Unified browser search across modules, assets, and technologies.

nodes.label: Source Files Index
nodes.description: Future searchable map of important source files, ownership, and runtime hooks.

nodes.label: Documents Index
nodes.description: Future searchable map of design docs, manuals, notes, and lore documents.

edges.reason: Knowledge search is surfaced in the Studio dashboard.
Asset records use the common Knowledge Engine indexing model.
Technology records use the common Knowledge Engine indexing model.
Creator tools produce reusable assets for the Asset Library.
Creator tools rely on vetted web and creative technologies.
DataByteSprites needs tools for art, sprites, prompts, and characters.
Sprites, icons, lore files, and previews belong in a searchable asset system.
Game data, lore, codex, and implementation notes need searchable project memory.
Game architecture should use vetted local-first and commercial-safe technologies.
Experiments should be searchable and connected to decisions.
Simulations need reusable assets and fixtures.
Simulations should use vetted frameworks and local tools.
Module records feed the Knowledge Engine.
Asset records feed the Knowledge Engine.
Technology records feed the Knowledge Engine.
Global search loads the module index.
Global search loads the asset index.
Global search loads the technology index.
Source file indexing is the next code intelligence layer.
Document indexing is the next documentation intelligence layer.

highestRiskDependencies.risk: DataByteSprites will grow faster than its asset metadata unless asset capture is automated.
Search will remain metadata-only until important source files are indexed.
